ArchitectureNotes.non-goal: Do not preserve backward compatibility with pre-role vvoc schemas or continue exposing target-based model override UX once the role-based contract is introduced. Do not track all subagents in the workflow state machine in v1. Do not persist workflow state across process restarts in v1.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-1: Config file mutation must stay conservative because users may already have hand-maintained JSONC with comments.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-3: Shared workflows may reference custom roles that are not assigned on another machine, so runtime role resolution must fail loudly and point at the offending config field.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-4: Provider-level alias models such as `openai/vv-gpt-5.4-xhigh` remain a separate lower-level mechanism, so the role system must compose with provider patch commands without silently requiring them.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-5: LLM may not reliably produce strict top-block protocol fields, so the parser must be tolerant of minor formatting variations while remaining strict on field presence and value validity.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-6: Rename of tracked subagents from `implementer/spec-reviewer/code-reviewer` to `vv-implementer/vv-spec-reviewer/vv-code-reviewer` is a breaking change for any external references to old names.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-7: Syncing `default_agent` to `vv-controller` is intentionally managed behavior and may override a user's hand-selected default primary agent.

contract.purpose: Define built-in role IDs, `vv-role:*` reference syntax, concrete model-selection parsing, canonical role assignment normalization, shared role resolution helpers, and built-in agent-to-role mappings.

export-isRoleReference.PURPOSE: Check whether a model string uses the `vv-role:*` reference syntax.

export-parseModelSelection.PURPOSE: Parse `provider/model[:variant]` selections used by canonical role assignments.

export-resolveRoleReference.PURPOSE: Resolve a `vv-role:*` reference against the canonical role map and return the concrete model selection.

export-getBuiltInRoleBindings.PURPOSE: Return the hard-coded built-in role mapping used by managed agents and OpenCode defaults.

notes.note-2: Custom roles are allowed, but roles do not resolve transitively to other roles in v1 of this redesign.

contract.purpose: Resolve OpenCode and canonical vvoc config paths, edit JSONC safely, pin the current vvoc package version in OpenCode config, write role-reference based OpenCode root defaults, built-in `explore`, managed default-agent/command entries, and managed agents conservatively, render canonical vvoc schema v3, refresh built-in role presets, and inspect installation state.

export-resolvePaths.PURPOSE: Resolve OpenCode config paths plus the canonical vvoc.json path for the selected scope.

export-ensurePackageInstalled.PURPOSE: Ensure the npm package is registered in OpenCode config with a pinned package version.

export-syncManagedAgentRegistrations.PURPOSE: Ensure OpenCode config contains root default model refs, `default_agent: vv-controller`, managed `/vv-plan` and `/vv-review` command definitions, plus built-in `explore` and managed agent registrations wired to `vv-role:*` references.

export-syncVvocConfig.PURPOSE: Rewrite the canonical vvoc.json document as schema v3 while preserving valid current role-based values and refreshing managed `vv-*` role presets.

target.source: src/lib/opencode.ts + src/lib/package.ts + src/lib/vvoc-config.ts + src/lib/model-roles.ts + src/lib/vvoc-paths.ts + schemas/vvoc/v3.json

notes.note-1: Main OpenCode config edits should preserve comments when only the plugin array, role references, default agent, managed commands, or managed agent registrations change.
notes.note-2: Pre-role vvoc schemas are rejected instead of migrated.

contract.purpose: Describe vvoc-managed OpenCode agents, resolve managed prompt paths, and load bundled or scoped prompt text for managed agent registrations. `vv-controller` is the managed default primary, `vv-analyst` and `vv-architect` are plan-writing subagents, and tracked subagents follow the `vv-*` naming convention.

export-MANAGED_OPENCODE_AGENTS.PURPOSE: Canonical managed OpenCode agent definitions used for bundled registration.

export-loadManagedAgentPromptTemplate.PURPOSE: Load the bundled prompt template for a managed vvoc agent.

export-loadManagedAgentPromptText.PURPOSE: Load managed prompt text from project or global vvoc config roots.

notes.note-1: Managed prompts resolve from project-local `.vvoc/agents/` first, then the global vvoc agents directory, while bundled templates remain the installation baseline.
notes.note-2: Tracked subagents are `vv-implementer`, `vv-spec-reviewer`, `vv-code-reviewer`. Prompt files follow the same naming. `vv-analyst` and `vv-architect` are untracked and may write only `.vvoc/plans/**`.
notes.note-3: `vv-controller` is a managed primary agent. `enhancer` remains a separate managed helper primary.

contract.purpose: Expose init, install, sync, status, doctor, version, role, preset, plugin list, config validate, patch-provider, completion, guardian config, and upgrade subcommands through citty while delegating role-aware config mutation to the helper layer.

export-init.PURPOSE: Bootstrap package registration and role-based config scaffolding (interactive by default).

export-sync.PURPOSE: Refresh canonical vvoc.json, role presets, managed prompts, and role-reference OpenCode config.

export-doctor.PURPOSE: Diagnose installation drift, unsupported vvoc schema versions, invalid role assignments, and unresolved role references.

notes.note-1: The `vvoc agent` command tree is removed as part of the hard cut to role-based model semantics.

contract.purpose: Resolve `vv-role:*` references inside OpenCode root model fields plus agent and command definitions during plugin config hooks so shared workflows bind semantic model intent to user-local provider choices at startup time.

param.name: Hooks
param.type: OpenCode plugin hooks with config mutation

export-ModelRolesPlugin.PURPOSE: Register a config hook that resolves `vv-role:*` references in supported OpenCode config fields.

notes.note-1: The plugin resolves `model`, `small_model`, `agent.*.model`, and `command.*.model` when they use `vv-role:*` syntax.

contract.purpose: Review OpenCode permission requests with a constrained Guardian agent whose runtime model resolves locally from the built-in `fast` role, while keeping safe fallback behavior and explicit threshold control.

export-GuardianPlugin.PURPOSE: Register Guardian hooks, config agent, built-in-role model selection, and permission review flow.

notes.note-1: Guardian no longer owns model fields in canonical vvoc config; only its non-model runtime policy remains there.

contract.purpose: Inject reusable vvoc system guidance into primary chat sessions while skipping known subagents, using agent-aware message hooks instead of the global system transform hook.

param.name: Hooks
param.type: OpenCode plugin hooks with config-aware chat.message mutation

export-SystemContextInjectionPlugin.PURPOSE: Inject main-session guidance such as proactive explore usage into `message.system` for eligible primary agents.

contract.purpose: Redact secrets from provider-bound payloads and restore placeholders after completions and before local tool execution.

export-SecretsRedactionPlugin.PURPOSE: Register secrets-redaction hooks for chat, completions, and tool execution.

contract.purpose: List, set, and unset semantic role assignments in canonical vvoc.json for both built-in and user-defined roles.

param.name: stdout
param.type: terminal output with role inventory or write summary

export-listRoles.PURPOSE: Render built-in and custom role assignments from canonical vvoc.json.

export-setRole.PURPOSE: Write a normalized concrete model selection for the selected role.

export-unsetRole.PURPOSE: Remove a custom role assignment without allowing built-in role deletion.

contract.purpose: List, show, and apply declarative named role presets stored in canonical vvoc.json without mutating OpenCode config directly.

export-resolvePreset.PURPOSE: Validate a preset name against the current canonical vvoc.json preset map.

export-applyPreset.PURPOSE: Apply only the listed role assignments through canonical vvoc config writes.

notes.note-1: Built-in presets remain `vv-openai`, `vv-zai`, `vv-minimax`, and `vv-deepseek` but now assign roles instead of target overrides.

contract.purpose: Validate canonical vvoc.json against the role-based vvoc JSON Schema v3 with human-readable error reporting and no compatibility mode for older schemas.

param.name: stdout
param.type: terminal output with pass/fail status for vvoc.json

export-validateVvocConfig.PURPOSE: Parse vvoc.json and report schema or semantic violations for roles and presets.

contract.purpose: Display all installed OpenCode plugins with their status (enabled/disabled) and source paths.

export-listPlugins.PURPOSE: Scan OpenCode config and display plugin list with status.

notes.note-1: Extended to show a vvoc-status column (enabled/disabled) based on the vvoc.json `plugins` section.

contract.purpose: Add `vvoc plugin enable <name>` and `vvoc plugin disable <name>` subcommands that mutate the `plugins` section in canonical vvoc.json.

param.name: stdout
param.type: terminal output with confirmation or error

export-enablePlugin.PURPOSE: Set plugins[<name>] = true in canonical vvoc.json.

export-disablePlugin.PURPOSE: Set plugins[<name>] = false in canonical vvoc.json.

notes.note-1: Validates plugin name against PLUGIN_TOGGLE_NAMES before writing.
notes.note-2: Prints restart instruction after successful write.

contract.purpose: Apply global OpenCode patch presets that rewrite provider.options.baseURL or merge provider-specific alias-model config under `provider` conservatively, without overwriting root OpenCode model fields that are now role-reference based.

param.name: WriteResult
param.type: created | updated | kept for global opencode.json/jsonc

export-resolvePatchProviderPreset.PURPOSE: Map a supported preset like stepfun-ai, zai, or openai to concrete OpenCode provider-object writes.

export-applyPatchProviderPreset.PURPOSE: Resolve global OpenCode config paths and apply the preset without mutating role-reference root defaults.

contract.purpose: Auto-detect the current shell and install vvoc completion files idempotently for bash, zsh, and fish, including patch-provider presets, role commands, role IDs, and preset names.

export-default.PURPOSE: Install shell completions for the detected shell.

export-detectShell.PURPOSE: Detect the active shell from environment variables.

contract.purpose: Interactive project initialization that registers @osovv/vv-opencode in the OpenCode plugin array, scaffolds managed prompts, seeds canonical role assignments, and writes role-reference based OpenCode config defaults. Uses @clack/prompts for TTY prompts.
Check npm for a newer vvoc release, install it globally with Bun, and trigger the default global sync flow from a fresh subprocess.

param.name: stdout
param.type: upgrade status, optional changelog, install progress, and sync results

export-runUpgradeFlow.PURPOSE: Execute the full global-only upgrade flow and warn instead of failing when post-install sync cannot complete.

export-fetchLatestVersion.PURPOSE: Query npm for the latest @osovv/vv-opencode version.

export-fetchChangelog.PURPOSE: Fetch and display best-effort release notes for the latest version.

contract.purpose: Define the tracked subagent result protocol grammar, parse strict top-block fields from subagent output, validate VVOC_STATUS per agent type, and extract VVOC_WORK_ITEM_ID from prompt headers.

export-TRACKED_SUBAGENT_NAMES.PURPOSE: Ordered list of tracked subagent names: vv-implementer, vv-spec-reviewer, vv-code-reviewer.

export-parseResultBlock.PURPOSE: Parse the strict top block from tracked subagent output.

export-validateStatusForAgent.PURPOSE: Validate a parsed status against the allowed set for the agent.

export-parseWorkItemHeader.PURPOSE: Extract VVOC_WORK_ITEM_ID from the first meaningful line of a prompt.

export-isTrackedSubagent.PURPOSE: Check whether a subagent name is in the tracked set.

notes.note-1: Allowed statuses per agent: vv-implementer={DONE, DONE_WITH_CONCERNS, NEEDS_CONTEXT, BLOCKED}; vv-spec-reviewer={PASS, FAIL, NEEDS_CONTEXT}; vv-code-reviewer={PASS, FAIL, NEEDS_CONTEXT}.
VVOC_ROUTE is mandatory only for vv-implementer.

contract.purpose: In-memory work-item state store scoped per session, with idempotent open-by-key, route-aware state transitions including review-only starts, review counter tracking, and round-limit computation.

export-createWorkItemStore.PURPOSE: Create a new scoped in-memory work-item store.

export-openWorkItem.PURPOSE: Create or return an existing work item by idempotency key within a session.

export-getWorkItem.PURPOSE: Retrieve a work item by ID and session.

export-listWorkItems.PURPOSE: List all open work items for a session with current state.

export-transitionWorkItem.PURPOSE: Transition a work item to a new state with counter updates.

export-getReviewRound.PURPOSE: Compute the current review round as max(specReviewCount, codeReviewCount).

notes.note-2: Idempotency is keyed by (sessionId, key). Same key plus same title returns same ID. Same key plus different title is an error.
notes.note-3: State is in-memory only in v1 and does not persist across process restarts.

contract.purpose: Deterministic transition rules between work-item states, allowed next-agent resolution including review-only fresh reviewer starts, and loop-gate policy enforcement with a round limit of 2.

export-getNextState.PURPOSE: Compute the next work-item state given the current state and a tracked agent result status.

export-getAllowedNextAgent.PURPOSE: Return the allowed tracked agent for the current work-item state.

export-isAllowedTransition.PURPOSE: Check whether launching a specific tracked agent is valid from the current state.

export-shouldBlockRound.PURPOSE: Check whether the current review round exceeds the allowed limit of 2.

notes.note-1: Implementation workflow: open/awaiting_implementer → vv-implementer → awaiting_spec_review → vv-spec-reviewer → awaiting_code_review → vv-code-reviewer → ready_to_close.
notes.note-1a: Review-only workflow may start a fresh open work item directly with vv-spec-reviewer or vv-code-reviewer.
notes.note-2: FAIL from reviewers routes back to awaiting_implementer. NEEDS_CONTEXT and BLOCKED are hard stops.
notes.note-3: Round limit uses `max(specReviewCount, codeReviewCount)`. Rounds 1 and 2 allowed, round 3 blocked.

contract.purpose: Custom tool definitions for work_item_open (batch-based with idempotency keys), work_item_list, and work_item_close that wrap the in-memory state store and return structured results including VVOC_WORK_ITEM_ID headers.

param.name: toolResult
param.type: structured JSON with items, state, or close confirmation

export-createWorkItemOpenTool.PURPOSE: Create the work_item_open custom tool handler with batch support.

export-createWorkItemListTool.PURPOSE: Create the work_item_list custom tool handler.

export-createWorkItemCloseTool.PURPOSE: Create the work_item_close custom tool handler.

contract.purpose: Register workflow tools, enforce tracked subagent launch validation via tool.execute.before on the task tool, parse tracked subagent results via tool.execute.after, and inject workflow protocol guidance into controller/eligible primary sessions via chat.message.

param.name: Hooks
param.type: OpenCode plugin hooks with custom tools, tool interception, and chat.message mutation

export-WorkflowPlugin.PURPOSE: Main plugin factory that registers workflow tools, launch enforcement hooks, result parsing hooks, and primary-session workflow guidance.

notes.note-1: Launch validation intercepts `task` in tool.execute.before and inspects subagent_type + prompt for tracked agents only.
notes.note-2: Result parsing intercepts `task` in tool.execute.after and parses the strict top block from tracked subagent output.
notes.note-3: Primary-session guidance is injected via chat.message, not through global system transform, to preserve agent filtering. `vv-controller` is eligible, while helper primary `enhancer` and known subagents are skipped.
notes.note-4: Untracked task launches pass through unaffected.

contract.purpose: Define supported vvoc model target IDs and shared model override validation helpers.

export-SPECIAL_AGENT_NAMES.PURPOSE: Target IDs using provider/model syntax in canonical vvoc config.

export-OPENCODE_DEFAULT_MODEL_TARGETS.PURPOSE: Target IDs mapping to top-level OpenCode model fields.

export-CONFIGURABLE_OPENCODE_AGENTS.PURPOSE: Built-in OpenCode agent IDs vvoc can override.

export-normalizeModelTargetOverride.PURPOSE: Validate and canonicalize stored model override strings.

export-formatAgentModel.PURPOSE: Format a model string for CLI output.

contract.purpose: Define the canonical plugin toggle names, the `plugins` section shape in vvoc.json, default-all-true values, and a shared `isPluginEnabled()` helper that each plugin factory can call at load time.

param.name: boolean
param.type: whether the plugin is enabled in vvoc.json

export-PLUGIN_TOGGLE_NAMES.PURPOSE: Canonical list of vvoc-managed plugin names: guardian, hashline-edit, model-roles, system-context-injection, workflow, secrets-redaction.

export-createDefaultPluginToggleConfig.PURPOSE: Return a Record<string, boolean> with all known plugins set to true.

export-isPluginEnabled.PURPOSE: Read the canonical vvoc.json and return whether the named plugin is enabled. If the plugins section is absent, default to true.

notes.note-1: Plugin names are kebab-case matching the OpenCode sub-path export convention: guardian, hashline-edit, model-roles, system-context-injection, workflow, secrets-redaction.
notes.note-2: If vvoc.json cannot be read or the `plugins` section is missing, return true (backward compatibility).

contract.purpose: Define the canonical managed vvoc preset registry for config seeding, sync refresh, and shell completion.

export-isBuiltinVvocPresetName.PURPOSE: Check whether a preset name is managed.

contract.purpose: Replace OpenCode's default edit tool with a hash-anchored variant and rewrite read output to stable line#hash#anchor rows so stale edits fail before file corruption.

export-HashlineEditPlugin.PURPOSE: Register hash-anchored edit tool override and post-read output enhancer.

contract.purpose: Recognize resumable OpenCode task-result envelopes, build strict repair prompts, and resume the same child session once to repair malformed tracked workflow outputs.

export-unwrapResumableTaskResult.PURPOSE: Extract inner tracked result text from recognized OpenCode task envelopes.

export-attemptTrackedResultRepair.PURPOSE: Resume the same child session once and return repaired output.

DF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.NAME: PluginInstall
DF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.step-1: Resolve scope-specific OpenCode config paths plus the canonical global vvoc.json path.
DF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.step-2: Ensure `@osovv/vv-opencode@<installed-version>` exists in the OpenCode `plugin` array.
DF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.step-3: Sync managed agent and command registrations so OpenCode root defaults, `default_agent`, built-in `explore`, managed `/vv-plan` and `/vv-review` commands, and managed agents point at vvoc-owned canonical values.
DF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.step-4: Create a fully seeded canonical vvoc schema v3 document with built-in roles and managed `vv-*` role presets.
DF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.evidence: `vvoc status`, parsed config content, and role-reference OpenCode config after install.

DF-CONFIG-SYNC.NAME: ManagedConfigSync
DF-CONFIG-SYNC.step-1: Inspect canonical vvoc.json and reject unsupported pre-role schema versions.
DF-CONFIG-SYNC.step-3: Rewrite vvoc.json canonically while preserving valid role assignments and refreshing managed `vv-*` role presets.
DF-CONFIG-SYNC.step-4: Rewrite OpenCode root defaults, `default_agent`, managed commands, built-in `explore`, and managed agents back to canonical values if drift occurred.
DF-CONFIG-SYNC.evidence: Stable rewritten vvoc.json, scope-correct managed prompt sync, default controller selection, managed command entries, and role-reference OpenCode config.

DF-ROLE-MANAGEMENT.NAME: RoleManagement
DF-ROLE-MANAGEMENT.step-1: Load or create the canonical vvoc schema v3 document.
DF-ROLE-MANAGEMENT.step-2: Validate the requested role ID and normalize any concrete `provider/model[:variant]` assignment.
DF-ROLE-MANAGEMENT.step-3: Write the updated role map or render the current role inventory.
DF-ROLE-MANAGEMENT.evidence: Built-in roles are always present, valid custom roles round-trip cleanly, and builtin role deletion is blocked.

DF-ROLE-RESOLUTION.NAME: RoleResolution
DF-ROLE-RESOLUTION.step-1: Load the canonical role map from global vvoc.json.
DF-ROLE-RESOLUTION.step-2: Walk supported OpenCode model-bearing fields and detect `vv-role:*` references.
DF-ROLE-RESOLUTION.step-3: Resolve each role reference to a concrete model selection, splitting `provider/model:variant` into native `model` plus `variant` fields where the target supports variants.
DF-ROLE-RESOLUTION.step-4: Throw an explicit error for unknown roles or unsupported concrete assignments instead of silently falling back.
DF-ROLE-RESOLUTION.evidence: Agent, command, and root model fields materialize user-local model choices from semantic role references.

DF-PRESET-SWITCH.NAME: PresetSwitch
DF-PRESET-SWITCH.step-1: Read configured presets from canonical vvoc.json and validate the requested preset name.
DF-PRESET-SWITCH.step-2: Normalize the preset's concrete role assignments.
DF-PRESET-SWITCH.step-3: Apply only the listed role assignments to canonical vvoc.json.
DF-PRESET-SWITCH.step-4: Leave OpenCode config untouched because it already points at stable `vv-role:*` references.
DF-PRESET-SWITCH.evidence: Selected roles change, unlisted roles remain untouched, and CLI output prints a per-role summary.

DF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.NAME: GuardianPermissionReview
DF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.step-0: Resolve the built-in `fast` role to the concrete Guardian runtime model before registering the hidden subagent.
DF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.step-1: Capture relevant tool and command intent for the session.
DF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.step-2: Build a bounded transcript and planned action prompt.
DF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.step-3: Run Guardian, interpret risk, and auto-reply only when below threshold.
DF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.evidence: Guardian logs, toasts, and permission reply behavior inside OpenCode.

DF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT.NAME: SystemContextInjection
DF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT.step-1: Build the known-subagent set from built-ins, plugin-managed agents, and config-defined subagents.
DF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT.step-2: Inspect the resolved agent name on each outgoing chat message.
DF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT.step-3: Append reusable vvoc system guidance only for eligible primary agents.
DF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT.step-4: Skip known subagents and internal helper agents so their prompts stay isolated.
DF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT.evidence: Primary agents receive one reusable guidance block; known subagents do not inherit it.

DF-SECRETS-REDACTION.NAME: SecretsRedaction
DF-SECRETS-REDACTION.TRIGGER: provider-bound request or tool execution with secrets present
DF-SECRETS-REDACTION.step-1: Load the secrets-redaction section from canonical vvoc.json with environment-variable substitution.
DF-SECRETS-REDACTION.step-2: Replace detected secrets in model-bound chat payloads with stable session placeholders.
DF-SECRETS-REDACTION.step-3: Restore placeholders in model completions and in tool arguments before local execution.
DF-SECRETS-REDACTION.step-4: Preserve session-local placeholder mappings with TTL and bounded cache behavior.
DF-SECRETS-REDACTION.evidence: Provider-bound payloads are redacted, while local tool execution and user-visible responses receive restored values where needed.

DF-PLUGIN-LIST.NAME: PluginList
DF-PLUGIN-LIST.step-1: Resolve OpenCode config path for the current scope.
DF-PLUGIN-LIST.step-2: Parse plugin array and extract each plugin's source.
DF-PLUGIN-LIST.step-3: Display plugin table with name, source, and enabled status.
DF-PLUGIN-LIST.evidence: Correct plugin table when plugins are installed, and graceful empty state when none are configured.

DF-CONFIG-VALIDATE.NAME: ConfigValidate
DF-CONFIG-VALIDATE.step-2: Parse the file with strict JSON rules.
DF-CONFIG-VALIDATE.step-3: Validate required fields, built-in role presence, and preset role assignments against the versioned vvoc JSON Schema v3.
DF-CONFIG-VALIDATE.step-4: Reject unsupported vvoc schema versions instead of migrating them.
DF-CONFIG-VALIDATE.evidence: Invalid JSON is caught and reported with line/column info; valid canonical role-based configs pass; older schemas fail loudly.

DF-COMPLETION.NAME: ShellCompletion
DF-COMPLETION.step-1: Detect the current shell from environment variables.
DF-COMPLETION.step-2: Generate shell-specific completion content for bash, zsh, or fish, including `role` subcommands and built-in role IDs.
DF-COMPLETION.step-3: Write the completion file to the shell-specific location.
DF-COMPLETION.step-4: Patch shell init files idempotently when the shell needs an explicit loader.
DF-COMPLETION.evidence: Installed completion files match the real vvoc command tree and role-centric subcommand surface.

DF-PLUGIN-TOGGLE.NAME: PluginToggle
DF-PLUGIN-TOGGLE.TRIGGER: vvoc plugin enable <name> | vvoc plugin disable <name>
DF-PLUGIN-TOGGLE.step-4: Set plugins[name] = true/false in the parsed config.
DF-PLUGIN-TOGGLE.step-6: Print confirmation with a reminder to restart the session.
DF-PLUGIN-TOGGLE.evidence: Config round-trips cleanly, known names are accepted, unknown names are rejected, and the change is visible in `vvoc plugin list` on the next read.

DF-PLUGIN-TOGGLE-CHECK.NAME: PluginToggleCheck
DF-PLUGIN-TOGGLE-CHECK.step-1: Plugin factory is called by OpenCode at startup.
DF-PLUGIN-TOGGLE-CHECK.step-3: Call isPluginEnabled(pluginName) which checks the `plugins` section.
DF-PLUGIN-TOGGLE-CHECK.step-4: If false, return {} (empty hooks) and emit a log message.
DF-PLUGIN-TOGGLE-CHECK.step-5: If true, proceed with normal initialization and hook registration.
DF-PLUGIN-TOGGLE-CHECK.evidence: Disabled plugins do not register any hooks. Enabled plugins behave identically to before the toggle was added.

DF-INIT.NAME: InteractiveInit
DF-INIT.step-1: Check --non-interactive flag. If absent and TTY detected, enter interactive mode with @clack/prompts.
DF-INIT.step-2: Prompt for scope selection (global/project) if interactive.
DF-INIT.step-3: Write initial managed prompts, managed OpenCode registrations, and canonical built-in role assignments.
DF-INIT.step-4: Ensure OpenCode root defaults plus built-in `explore` and managed agents point at canonical `vv-role:*` references.
DF-INIT.evidence: Canonical config is valid JSON after init; Ctrl+C aborts cleanly; re-running init is idempotent; non-interactive mode works without TTY.

DF-UPGRADE.NAME: UpgradeCheck
DF-UPGRADE.step-1: Query npm registry for @osovv/vv-opencode dist-tags latest.
DF-UPGRADE.step-3: If newer, fetch best-effort changelog context and install `@osovv/vv-opencode@<latest>` globally with `bun add -g`.
DF-UPGRADE.step-4: After a successful install, launch `vvoc sync` in a fresh subprocess so the new package version and sync logic are used.
DF-UPGRADE.step-5: If sync cannot run or exits non-zero, keep the upgrade successful but warn the user to run `vvoc sync` manually.
DF-UPGRADE.evidence: Network failure is handled gracefully; already-latest case prints OK; install failures stop before sync; successful installs trigger the default global sync flow.

DF-RENAME-AGENTS.NAME: TrackedAgentRename
DF-RENAME-AGENTS.step-1: Rename managed subagent definitions from implementer, spec-reviewer, code-reviewer to vv-implementer, vv-spec-reviewer, vv-code-reviewer in managed-agents metadata.
DF-RENAME-AGENTS.step-2: Rename prompt template files from implementer.md, spec-reviewer.md, code-reviewer.md to vv-implementer.md, vv-spec-reviewer.md, vv-code-reviewer.md.
DF-RENAME-AGENTS.step-3: Update managed OpenCode agent registrations to use the new vv-* names and prompt file references.
DF-RENAME-AGENTS.step-4: Update model-roles managed agent role bindings to use vv-* keys.
DF-RENAME-AGENTS.step-5: Update system-context-injection known subagent set to include vv-* names.
DF-RENAME-AGENTS.evidence: Install and sync scaffold renamed tracked agents with vv-* names and prompt file references. Prompt lookup works for renamed files. All tests referencing managed agent registration pass.

DF-WORKFLOW-TOOLS.NAME: WorkItemTools
DF-WORKFLOW-TOOLS.step-1: Receive tool call with structured input from main session.
DF-WORKFLOW-TOOLS.step-2: For work_item_open, create or return existing items by idempotency key, generating wi-{N} IDs and VVOC_WORK_ITEM_ID headers.
DF-WORKFLOW-TOOLS.step-3: For work_item_list, return all open items with current state, counters, and review round.
DF-WORKFLOW-TOOLS.step-4: For work_item_close, validate the item exists and is open, then set state to closed with timestamp.
DF-WORKFLOW-TOOLS.evidence: Items are created with deterministic IDs, same key plus same title returns same ID, same key plus different title fails, list reflects current state, close is explicit.

DF-LAUNCH-ENFORCEMENT.NAME: LaunchEnforcement
DF-LAUNCH-ENFORCEMENT.step-1: Inspect output.args.subagent_type to check if it is a tracked agent.
DF-LAUNCH-ENFORCEMENT.step-2: If not tracked, allow the launch to proceed without interference.
DF-LAUNCH-ENFORCEMENT.step-3: If tracked, inspect output.args.prompt for a valid first-line VVOC_WORK_ITEM_ID header.
DF-LAUNCH-ENFORCEMENT.step-4: Validate the work item exists and is open in the current session.
DF-LAUNCH-ENFORCEMENT.step-5: Validate the tracked agent is the allowed next agent for the current work-item state.
DF-LAUNCH-ENFORCEMENT.step-6: Validate the review round limit is not exceeded.
DF-LAUNCH-ENFORCEMENT.step-7: Reject the launch with a clear error message if any validation fails.
DF-LAUNCH-ENFORCEMENT.evidence: Tracked launches without valid header are rejected, unknown items are rejected, invalid transitions are blocked, round overflow is blocked, untracked launches pass through.

DF-RESULT-PARSING.NAME: ResultParsing
DF-RESULT-PARSING.step-1: Inspect tracked subagent output after task completion.
DF-RESULT-PARSING.step-2: Parse the strict top block: VVOC_WORK_ITEM_ID, VVOC_STATUS, and optional VVOC_ROUTE.
DF-RESULT-PARSING.step-3: Validate work-item identity matches the launched item.
DF-RESULT-PARSING.step-4: Validate the status is allowed for the agent type.
DF-RESULT-PARSING.step-5: Update counters: implementerCount, specReviewCount, codeReviewCount as appropriate.
DF-RESULT-PARSING.step-6: Transition work-item state based on the result.
DF-RESULT-PARSING.step-7: On NEEDS_CONTEXT or BLOCKED, set state to hard-stop and mark halted.
DF-RESULT-PARSING.step-8: On protocol error, halt automation and return error to main session.
DF-RESULT-PARSING.evidence: Valid results update state correctly, malformed results fail closed, NEEDS_CONTEXT returns control to main session, counters increment deterministically.

DF-LOOP-GATE.NAME: LoopGate
DF-LOOP-GATE.step-1: Compute reviewRound = max(specReviewCount, codeReviewCount) for the target work item.
DF-LOOP-GATE.step-3: Before entering round 3, reject the next tracked launch.
DF-LOOP-GATE.step-4: Return explicit next-action guidance: gather context, split the item, close the item, involve the user, or run an untracked investigation.
DF-LOOP-GATE.evidence: Repeated review loops stop deterministically at round 3. No semantic heuristics. Main session can inspect state and recover explicitly.

DF-WORKFLOW-GUIDANCE.NAME: WorkflowGuidance
DF-WORKFLOW-GUIDANCE.step-1: Inspect the resolved agent name on each outgoing chat message.
DF-WORKFLOW-GUIDANCE.step-2: If the agent is a known subagent, skip injection.
DF-WORKFLOW-GUIDANCE.step-3: If the agent is eligible for primary session injection, append workflow protocol guidance covering: work-item opening, header usage, hard-stop handling, state inspection, and loop-gate behavior.
DF-WORKFLOW-GUIDANCE.evidence: Eligible orchestrating primary agents receive workflow protocol guidance. Known subagents and helper primaries such as enhancer do not inherit it.

Phase-1.name: CLI Foundation
Phase-1.goal: Establish safe config mutation helpers and wire them into a usable Bun CLI.
Phase-1.step-1: Implement path resolution, JSONC OpenCode edits, canonical vvoc.json helpers, and installation inspection.
Phase-1.step-2: Expose install, sync, status, doctor, and Guardian config commands.

Phase-2.name: Guardian Packaging
Phase-2.goal: Move the existing standalone Guardian plugin into the repository and export it from the package.
Phase-2.step-1: Port Guardian and move runtime config to the canonical vvoc.json path.

Phase-4.name: CLI Enhancement Suite
Phase-4.goal: Add preset/configuration helpers and developer-experience commands to improve setup, inspection, and switching flows.
Phase-4.step-1: Validate canonical vvoc.json with human-readable schema errors.
Phase-4.step-2: List installed plugins with status, enabled/disabled, and source path.
Phase-4.step-3: Apply supported OpenCode patch presets conservatively to the global OpenCode config.
Phase-4.step-4: List, inspect, and apply named presets from canonical vvoc.json.
Phase-4.step-5: Install shell completions for bash, zsh, and fish with shell auto-detection.
Phase-4.step-6: Interactive project setup with prompts for config location and plugin selection.
Phase-4.step-7: Check npm for updates, install the global package with Bun, and run post-install sync from a fresh subprocess.

Phase-5.name: Session Guidance And Safety
Phase-5.goal: Inject reusable main-session guidance and keep provider-bound payloads free of raw secrets.
Phase-5.step-1: Append reusable vvoc system guidance only for eligible primary agents via `chat.message`.
Phase-5.step-2: Redact secrets before LLM requests and restore placeholders after completion and before tool execution.

Phase-6.name: Role-Based Model Semantics
Phase-6.goal: Replace target-based model overrides with semantic role assignment, runtime `vv-role:*` resolution, and role-centric CLI workflows.
Phase-6.step-1: Implement the shared role contract, built-in role bindings, and concrete model-selection parsing helpers.
Phase-6.step-2: Upgrade canonical vvoc config to schema v3, seed built-in roles, remove target-based config fields, and keep OpenCode root defaults plus built-in `explore` and managed agents pinned to `vv-role:*` references.
Phase-6.step-3: Resolve role references at startup for supported OpenCode config fields and fail loudly for unknown roles.
Phase-6.step-4: Move Guardian model selection to the built-in role system without depending on plugin hook ordering.
Phase-6.step-6: Introduce the role command tree and remove the legacy `agent` override UX.
Phase-6.step-7: Convert built-in and user presets from target-based writes to role assignment writes only.
Phase-6.step-8: Keep provider alias patching as a lower-level helper for concrete role assignments without mutating root OpenCode model fields.
Phase-6.step-9: Update completion output for the new role command surface and built-in role IDs.
Phase-6.step-10: Seed role-based defaults during install/init and surface role state in status/doctor output.

Phase-7.name: Tracked Agent Rename
Phase-7.goal: Rename tracked managed subagents to vv-* naming convention and update all references consistently.
Phase-7.step-1: Update managed subagent names from implementer, spec-reviewer, code-reviewer to vv-implementer, vv-spec-reviewer, vv-code-reviewer. Rename prompt template files.
Phase-7.step-2: Update managedAgents role bindings to use vv-* keys: vv-implementer, vv-spec-reviewer, vv-code-reviewer.
Phase-7.step-3: Update managed agent registration sync to write vv-* names and vv-* prompt file references while preserving user-owned legacy old-name agents.
Phase-7.step-4: Add vv-implementer, vv-spec-reviewer, vv-code-reviewer to the known subagent set so they stay excluded from primary-session guidance injection.
Phase-7.step-5: Confirm completion output has no legacy tracked-agent command surface; no additional vv-* completion delta is required.

Phase-8.name: Workflow Core
Phase-8.goal: Implement the protocol grammar, in-memory state store, transition rules, and work-item tools.
Phase-8.step-1: Implement protocol field parsing, case-sensitive status validation, duplicate-field rejection, and work-item header extraction.
Phase-8.step-2: Implement in-memory work-item store with idempotent open, state tracking, counter management, and sticky hard-stop protections.
Phase-8.step-3: Implement deterministic transition rules and round-limit enforcement.
Phase-8.step-4: Implement work_item_open, work_item_list, and work_item_close custom tool handlers with deterministic structured responses.

Phase-9.name: Workflow Plugin Integration
Phase-9.goal: Wire protocol, state, transitions, and tooling into the main WorkflowPlugin with enforcement hooks.
Phase-9.step-1: Create plugin entry point with tool registration, launch enforcement via tool.execute.before, result parsing via tool.execute.after, and orchestrating-primary guidance via chat.message.
Phase-9.step-2: Export WorkflowPlugin from package root and add ./plugins/workflow export path.

Phase-10.name: Prompt Template Updates
Phase-10.goal: Update tracked subagent prompt templates with strict top-block protocol requirements.
Phase-10.step-1: Update vv-implementer.md prompt template to require VVOC_WORK_ITEM_ID, VVOC_STATUS, VVOC_ROUTE top block.
Phase-10.step-2: Update vv-spec-reviewer.md prompt template to require VVOC_WORK_ITEM_ID and VVOC_STATUS top block.
Phase-10.step-3: Update vv-code-reviewer.md prompt template to require VVOC_WORK_ITEM_ID and VVOC_STATUS top block.

Phase-11.name: Documentation And Verification
Phase-11.goal: Fully document and verify the workflow protocol, rename, and enforcement.
Phase-11.step-1: Update README with vv-* managed subagent names, WorkflowPlugin coverage, workflow protocol, and work-item tool usage.
Phase-11.step-2: Confirm completion scripts still expose no legacy tracked-agent command surface; no workflow-specific completion changes are required.
Phase-11.step-3: Add targeted tests for protocol parsing, state transitions, launch enforcement, result parsing, hard-stop behavior, and loop gating.
Phase-11.step-4: Run full check, build, and pack:check to validate package integrity.

Phase-12.name: Plugin Runtime Toggle
Phase-12.goal: Add `plugins` section to canonical vvoc.json, shared `isPluginEnabled()` utility, CLI enable/disable commands, and per-plugin load-time checks so every vvoc-managed plugin can be toggled at runtime.
Phase-12.step-1: Implement PLUGIN_TOGGLE_NAMES, createDefaultPluginToggleConfig, isPluginEnabled helper, and update VvocConfig type with the plugins section.
Phase-12.step-2: Update vvoc schema v3 to add plugins section and remove enabled from secretsRedaction section. Update createDefaultVvocConfig to seed plugins all-true. Update syncVvocConfig to preserve existing plugins values.
Phase-12.step-3: Add vvoc plugin enable/disable CLI subcommands that validate plugin names and write to vvoc.json.
Phase-12.step-4: Extend plugin list to show a vvoc-status column reading from the vvoc.json plugins section.
Phase-12.step-5: Add isPluginEnabled check to GuardianPlugin factory, return {} when disabled.
Phase-12.step-6: Add isPluginEnabled check to HashlineEditPlugin factory, return {} when disabled.
Phase-12.step-7: Add isPluginEnabled check to ModelRolesPlugin factory, return {} when disabled.
Phase-12.step-8: Add isPluginEnabled check to SystemContextInjectionPlugin factory, return {} when disabled.
Phase-12.step-9: Add isPluginEnabled check to WorkflowPlugin factory, return {} when disabled.
Phase-12.step-10: Replace internal enabled check with isPluginEnabled call, remove enabled from SecretsRedactionConfig type and schema.
Phase-12.step-11: Run full check, build, and pack:check.

ExecutionPolicy.worker-owns: Only package source files, tests, and narrowly scoped documentation updates required by the approved module changes.

phase-7.notes: Pure rename across 7+ files. Existing test patterns apply. Gate-Phase-7 covers config, registration, and prompt file assertions.

phase-8.notes: Pure functions, in-memory state, no external deps. All scenarios are deterministic assertions. Gate-Phase-8 covers protocol, state, transitions, and tooling.

phase-9.notes: Depends on OpenCode plugin API surface for tool.execute.before/after. Requires manual API validation before autonomous execution. Gate-Phase-9 covers integration paths plus build/pack.

phase-10.notes: Text file updates with deterministic content checks.

phase-11.notes: Documentation sync, build and pack smoke checks. Gate-Phase-11 covers full suite.

phase-12.notes: New utility module, schema update, CLI additions, and per-plugin load checks. Each plugin check is a 2-line addition following a shared pattern. Gate-Phase-12 covers config, CLI, and per-plugin tests.

test-file-layout.workflow-test-scope: Covers M-WORKFLOW-PROTOCOL, M-WORKFLOW-STATE, M-WORKFLOW-TRANSITIONS, M-WORKFLOW-TOOLING, and M-PLUGIN-WORKFLOW in a single file following the existing plugin test pattern.
